reasoning: The informal statement asks to prove that cos(1°) is algebraic over ℚ. Since 1° equals π/180 radians, `cos (Real.pi / 180)` correctly represents cos(1°). The predicate `IsAlgebraic ℚ x` in Mathlib precisely means x is algebraic over ℚ, i.e., it is a root of some nonzero polynomial with rational coefficients. The Lean signature `IsAlgebraic ℚ (cos (Real.pi / 180))` captures exactly the informal claim with no additional or missing hypotheses, conclusions, or type changes.
